instructions: Review the official Frostgate arrival notice and list every deadline, deposit, document, or service requirement.
Select one verified housing or address path and calculate its first-week cash commitment.
Confirm one lawful income, training, public-service, or enterprise lead tied to strategic-minerals, sensor manufacturing, and cold-chain services.
Document a fallback through Frostgate Settlement Office and state the amount of cash that remains protected.
records.objective: Produce a verified first-week stabilization plan for Frostgate that resolves temporary heating allocation, a recognized address, and a verified winter transport route and preserves a minimum emergency reserve.
records.playerFacingDescription: Frostgate Settlement Office needs a practical first-week plan before it will clear your settlement support. You must resolve temporary heating allocation, a recognized address, and a verified winter transport route without spending the emergency reserve that protects you from an early setback.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit a four-part stabilization dossier: requirement checklist, selected address path, first-week cash plan, and verified fallback contact.

instructions: Record expected first-week income, required equipment or transport, and the earliest realistic payment date for each option.
Explain how heating costs, weather delays, and concentrated energy supply could reduce or delay each option’s value.
Reject any option that depends on an unavailable mechanic, unverified employer, or unlicensed transaction.
Choose one path and define a fallback trigger that would cause you to switch.
records.objective: Compare a supervised mineral-assay placement with a cold-chain logistics assistant role and select the path that best preserves liquidity under heating costs, weather delays, and concentrated energy supply.
records.playerFacingDescription: Two legitimate income paths are available in Frostgate: a supervised mineral-assay placement and a cold-chain logistics assistant role. Compare the real costs, timing, and risks before committing your limited starting capital.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit a side-by-side livelihood comparison with cash timing, required inputs, risk adjustment, selected path, and fallback trigger.

instructions: Define the customer problem inside strategic-minerals, sensor manufacturing, and cold-chain services and name the buyer or institution most likely to pay.
Use approved market, Store, Contract, or country information to support one demand claim.
Quantify or rank the downside caused by heating costs, weather delays, and concentrated energy supply.
Recommend a bounded first transaction or pilot that does not require unsupported features.
records.objective: Evaluate the commercial potential of precision sensing and mineral verification services in Frostgate and identify the most important downside created by heating costs, weather delays, and concentrated energy supply.
records.playerFacingDescription: A local operator wants a concise opportunity brief on precision sensing and mineral verification services. The brief must identify a credible buyer, a realistic constraint, and evidence that the opportunity is more than speculative hype.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit a market brief containing customer, problem, evidence, downside risk, and a bounded pilot recommendation.

instructions: Identify the affected product, service, Contract, or community need and the point where the dependency enters the chain.
State the disruption trigger, expected duration, and the inventory or cash exposure at risk.
Evaluate cryogenic ceramic and conventional sensor-board production for cost, quality, timing, and legal eligibility.
Define a recovery sequence that limits losses and avoids duplicate claims or unsupported inventory.
records.objective: Map the dependency on superconductive filament arriving through the northern freight corridor and test whether cryogenic ceramic and conventional sensor-board production can maintain a minimum viable service.
records.playerFacingDescription: A local supply chain relies on superconductive filament arriving through the northern freight corridor. A disruption would halt essential work, so the operator needs a lawful substitute plan before committing inventory or credit.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit a dependency map, disruption trigger, substitute comparison, and ordered recovery sequence.

instructions: Confirm the beneficiary, scope, and acceptance standard before beginning.
Use only approved public information, available inventory, and supported Contract evidence.
Record the completed work with a checklist, calculation, map, or verification log appropriate to the task.
Explain one measurable local benefit and one limitation that remains unresolved.
records.objective: Complete and document the following verified service in Frostgate: audit an emergency warming center’s supply list and transport access.
records.playerFacingDescription: Frostgate Settlement Office is commissioning a small public-service task: audit an emergency warming center’s supply list and transport access. The work must produce evidence that another resident or local operator can actually use.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit completion evidence, the beneficiary’s acceptance criteria, one measurable impact, and one unresolved limitation.

Evaluate refined alloy ingot and staged low-volume fabrication for cost, quality, timing, and legal eligibility.
Define a recovery sequence that limits losses and avoids duplicate claims or unsupported inventory.
records.objective: Map the dependency on machine steel billet delivered from a concentrated supplier and test whether refined alloy ingot and staged low-volume fabrication can maintain a minimum viable service.
records.playerFacingDescription: A local supply chain relies on machine steel billet delivered from a concentrated supplier. A disruption would halt essential work, so the operator needs a lawful substitute plan before committing inventory or credit.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit a dependency map, disruption trigger, substitute comparison, and ordered recovery sequence.

instructions: Confirm the beneficiary, scope, and acceptance standard before beginning.
Use only approved public information, available inventory, and supported Contract evidence.
Record the completed work with a checklist, calculation, map, or verification log appropriate to the task.
Explain one measurable local benefit and one limitation that remains unresolved.
records.objective: Complete and document the following verified service in Emberhall: prepare a material and labor estimate for a small public repair project.
records.playerFacingDescription: Emberhall Development Authority is commissioning a small public-service task: prepare a material and labor estimate for a small public repair project. The work must produce evidence that another resident or local operator can actually use.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit completion evidence, the beneficiary’s acceptance criteria, one measurable impact, and one unresolved limitation.

instructions: Review the official Ironhold arrival notice and list every deadline, deposit, document, or service requirement.
Select one verified housing or address path and calculate its first-week cash commitment.
Confirm one lawful income, training, public-service, or enterprise lead tied to mining, foundry work, heavy maintenance, and structural repair.
Document a fallback through Ironhold Technical Placement Office and state the amount of cash that remains protected.
records.objective: Produce a verified first-week stabilization plan for Ironhold that resolves technical placement, worker-housing eligibility, and verified safety credentials and preserves a minimum emergency reserve.
records.playerFacingDescription: Ironhold Technical Placement Office needs a practical first-week plan before it will clear your settlement support. You must resolve technical placement, worker-housing eligibility, and verified safety credentials without spending the emergency reserve that protects you from an early setback.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit a four-part stabilization dossier: requirement checklist, selected address path, first-week cash plan, and verified fallback contact.

instructions: Record expected first-week income, required equipment or transport, and the earliest realistic payment date for each option.
Explain how equipment downtime, housing tied to employment, and heavy-material shortages could reduce or delay each option’s value.
Reject any option that depends on an unavailable mechanic, unverified employer, or unlicensed transaction.
Choose one path and define a fallback trigger that would cause you to switch.
records.objective: Compare a supervised maintenance-rig service rotation with a structural inspection support assignment and select the path that best preserves liquidity under equipment downtime, housing tied to employment, and heavy-material shortages.
records.playerFacingDescription: Two legitimate income paths are available in Ironhold: a supervised maintenance-rig service rotation and a structural inspection support assignment. Compare the real costs, timing, and risks before committing your limited starting capital.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit a side-by-side livelihood comparison with cash timing, required inputs, risk adjustment, selected path, and fallback trigger.

instructions: Define the customer problem inside mining, foundry work, heavy maintenance, and structural repair and name the buyer or institution most likely to pay.
Use approved market, Store, Contract, or country information to support one demand claim.
Quantify or rank the downside caused by equipment downtime, housing tied to employment, and heavy-material shortages.
Recommend a bounded first transaction or pilot that does not require unsupported features.
records.objective: Evaluate the commercial potential of structural inspection and heavy-equipment maintenance in Ironhold and identify the most important downside created by equipment downtime, housing tied to employment, and heavy-material shortages.
records.playerFacingDescription: A local operator wants a concise opportunity brief on structural inspection and heavy-equipment maintenance. The brief must identify a credible buyer, a realistic constraint, and evidence that the opportunity is more than speculative hype.
records.submissionRequirement: Submit a market brief containing customer, problem, evidence, downside risk, and a bounded pilot recommendation.
